stall_name: Yu Mei Mei Shi (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Balmy morning with a spot of rain. This is the perfect antidote. Happy Holy Saturday all.
Fish soup for lunch today with lots of vegetables

The nasi lemak was a side we shared! Back to my mains, I love this fish soup here. Soup is full of umami and msg free and their fried fish is one of the best I’ve had. Had it with Yee Mee today and with extra greens, chye sim and bittergourd. So yummy. Fish soup cravings satisfied.
No msg and super fresh fish, my go to for comfort food

A few of us trooped over today to check out this fish soup stall in Havelock Food Centre that's been raved about for a while now by Burppler @blueskiescottonclouds. 'Yu Mei Mei Shi' (unit #01-15) has been open for 22 years and is actually rather well-known, especially among many taxi drivers. Karen, the stall owners' daughter is an ex-colleague and friend of Angeline's, and she'd quit her banking job to help her parents out. Being greedy, I couldn't stop myself from ordering a customised bowl of chunkily-cut red garoupa, fish roe, 'yubao' (creamy fish sac), fried fish and bittergourd. My bill came to $13.50 with a bowl of rice. Not cheap for hawker fare but I think it's worth it because of the quality of the ingredients. In spite of a light, clear appearance, the soup was impressive in depth and sweetness too. A must-have dip is the sambal belachan. Made by the stall owners themselves, it is spicy and extra appetising from the inclusion of lime juice. #burpplemajulahmakan
Back at my fav with extra fried fish and bittergourd.

google.google_name: Yu Mei Mei Shi 嶼美美食

reviews.author: WTHappening
reviews.text: Around 635am in the morning, the rice wasn't ready. So I got the thick bee hoon instead. Overall the meal was good, a heavy breakfast to start off the day.

reviews.author: Kai En
reviews.text: Great fish soup with quality ingredients. Soup is clean tasting with sole fish taste. Fish and prawns are fresh

reviews.author: Melvin L.
reviews.text:
Good effort but could be better as there is another fish soup nearby. And I tried this as it was same rating with more reviews.

Ordered double fish.. the battered was too thick for fish soup not fish and chips. Soup was nothing tat great. But ingredients did match the price.

reviews.author: AH Chen
reviews.text:
Standards dropped
Was there 4 years ago
Now the soup taste sugary and sweet
Fish fresh is ok.

reviews.author: Loong Choon See
reviews.text:
Amazing fish soup tucked away in the middle of old HDB flats.

Ordered the 双鱼汤: Soup was savory and light at the same time.  Well-balanced and not too salty. Fish was fresh and the occasional crunch of grinded salted fish bits in the soup was a nice touch!

stall_name: Western Hut (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: The fish is not so salty and it is crispy. The fries are fat, the kind that i like.

reviews.author: Mark Yeo
reviews.text: First time customer today. We pre ordered dinner for self-collect at 545pm. Our order comprise 3 chicken chops, 1 alio olio fish and 1 tomato (bologna) pasta fish. Each set came with a gravy / dressing that tasted great - it tasted like an apple sauce actually. The kids loved it as it was yummy. We encountered a friendly stall operator who put in effort and care in packing our dinner for take away. During the packing process, he said to us to be careful as the sauce was very hot and he packed the 5 small sauce containers into a separate plastic carrier bag.

reviews.author: Etree
reviews.text: Stall owner is a young gentleman who is accommodating and friendly. He allowed some changes to my order and generally quite a chill person. The well cooked chicken chop is light crispy on the top and tender on the inside. The sauce is special and goes very well. It's not exactly mushroom sauce but unique to this stall. The tomato spaghetti and coleslaw was ok, but not the best. Come here for the chicken chop.

reviews.author: Angel Song Li Qin
reviews.text:
Can't say much about the food as I've yet to try it. Was excited to try it today as I found an old school western food near my new home, went over after work around 5.45pm and was shocked. It was closed. But Google wrote that their operation hours is Monday to Friday, 12pm to 8pm.

Not fated to try it. Will try again next time during noon time if I'm on leave.

If anyone want to come during evening time, please be prepared that it might not be open.

reviews.author: Cheemeng Ong
reviews.text: Ordered Fish And Chips. Well batter fish to the crisp. Fresh fried fries. Even though I prefer old school type with a slice of lemon. Just wondering why there is a need of brown sauce for it. $7.50 , can’t ask for much on its quality. At least,not much of smell of the fish. Can be even better to make the fish taste better and juicy. Will come back again to try it chicken chop.

reviews.author: MJ
reviews.text: This chicken cutlet taste reminds me of the one I had when I was younger, almost 25 years back at Tanglin Halt. Amazing!

reviews: Small doesn't have egg... abit of heat but lacks body.
Flavourful laksa broth... Havelock Road FC always appears kinda weird...
Traditional Laksa With Cockles... Laksa.
Very Generous Serving Of Fat Hums!
Laksa With Cockles... tried it a year or two back...
Rich and Lemak Laksa... operated for close to fifteen years in Ghim Moh...

google.google_name: Guang Fa Laksa 廣發叻沙 (#01-08)

reviews.author: Kai Xuan Goh
reviews.text:
At first, the broth feels a little less lemak, a little thinner, but this is a bowl that will grow into you.
The flavour starts to deepen and get more fragrant, the beehoon well cooked, a little nice bite all the way. The ingredients are all standard good, the hum a standout, being plumper.
Towards the bottom of the bowl were hae bi, which was a nice touch and a flavour boost to the broth.
To summarize, this was a bowl that was as good as Sungei Road Laksa 10 years back, just lacking the charcoal notes in the broth.

reviews.author: KW Ang
reviews.text:
Have been planning to try this famous laksa stall for some time. Reached at about 12:40pm expecting long queue during lunchtime peak hours, but was surprised only 2 person in the queue, while the Char Keow Teow stall nearby has a long queue of more than 15 persons. I ordered $5 laksa. The broth was nice but the really small & shrivel cockles is a big let down. They did give many cockles, but there is no enjoyment eating many "meatless" and tasteless mini cockles. Perhaps that explain the "almost no queue" at the stall.
Conclusion: average laksa. Not for cockle lover, most laksa stall serves better cockles than this.

reviews.author: Jacob TAN
reviews.text:
One of the best laksa that you can find in Singapore. There is always a queue for this stall and he finishes selling very early, usually by 1 pm. The gravy is thick and rich in coconut flavour. Good mixture of ingredients or you can choose to just have cockles.
Service is slow as it is a one man operation.

reviews.author: Soo Hin Yeoh
reviews.text:
Definitely one of the best laksa stalls in Singapore 🇸🇬

Laksa portions are generous although wish we get more soup. The gravy is thick with strong prawn paste aroma. Prices have gone up from $3.00 to $3.50 and now $4.00 but still, highly recommended 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

Go in the mornings otherwise come before 1130am on a weekday, expect queue times of up to 30-min on peak hours.

reviews.author: 在地狼
reviews.text: The broth was watery and lacked the rich, lemak depth you’d expect, with the taste remaining flat throughout. Overall, I’ve had much better laksa elsewhere, this one was pretty average and definitely didn’t live up to the hype.

stall_name: Siak Ann Cooked Food (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Although the fact that this stall only opens at 3am would turn most people away... Siak Ann Curry Rice

reviews.author: Hatsune Miku
reviews.text: Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre is a pretty small food centre with a few hidden gems. Perhaps the most famous stall here is the char kway teow stall here which some say is one of the best in Singapore. The laksa here isnt bad either with their large servings of cockles. Cockle lovers should definitely take the time to taste the laksa here. The ngoh hiang here is also pretty decent, but waiting times may be a little long. Kueh Tutu is also sold here, and this is one of the local delicacies that is getting hard to find. The fish soup stall right at the end serves generous portions of fish also. Overall, this is a pretty well-rounded food centre. However, the place is a little old and shabby, and water leaks when there is rain. The carpark is a little small, and you may have trouble securing a parking lot. This is a food centre that is very well favoured with phv and taxi drivers, and with good reason too.

reviews.author: kelvin chan
reviews.text: Enjoyed Traditional Carrot Cake Chai Tau Kweh, stall fries the carrot cake with Chai Pu. Rare to get this version of the dish nowadays

reviews.author: Richard Khoo
reviews.text: Nice and affordable food. The prawn noodle is nice and value for money (big prawns). Du du kueh is nice too. Traditional hawker centre ambience.

reviews.author: Yap Hoe BENG
reviews.text:
May25, this is a small hawker Center with carpark. Not every stall is open but there r few famous stall here. Like the fish soup at the corner stall - very good. Also known is the BCM & CKT which we haven’t try yet.
Nov25, try again today.

reviews.author: L Chan
reviews.text:
A wonderful hawker centre full of old-school charm & heritage. Built in 1969 after the Bukit Ho Swee fire, this humble L-shaped centre has been serving the neighbourhood for decades. Some stalls trace their roots to the old Covent Garden estate.

Peaceful & full of heart — this is one of the last few hawker centres that still feels like a step back in time.

stall_name: Siam Square Mookata (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Best Value-for-money Mookata... Siam Square Mookata is probably one of the few places...
Authentic mookata... many outlets...
Late night cravings...

google.google_name: Siam Square Mookata - Best Mookata Restaurant in Singapore

reviews.author: gladys
reviews.text:
Food was good and reasonably priced! Uncle was very helpful and friendly, will come back again for the food and service

Went back again on friday (25th july) to eat again! bacon and cheese is a must try😋😋

reviews.author: Cybermon K S
reviews.text: BBQ buffet is the main highlight of this new restaurant near by the lake. 59 dollar for 2 people for food. The only restriction is for items priced 4 dollar and above. Rest all items in the menu including ice cream is unlimited. The quantity and quality of the meat is great. The meat or what ever we use for the bbq provided are very fresh and so it’s very tasty as well. The bbq stove is a gas stove and so the time to prepare the bbq is very less. It’s more of a instant food rather than traditional bbq. The staffs here are so friendly and very attentive. Two things which are the base of this bbq is the quality and taste of the sauce they provide and the base liquid or soup provided to boil in the bbq pit. It can be a good place for any kind of people like couples or even family. A very well recommended place.

reviews.author: David Sim
reviews.text: Quiet Sunday night supper at Siam Square Mookata. Nice food, quick service and friendly staff. Ample parking and free parking on Sunday. Price is reasonable and good variety.

reviews.author: Alvin Toh
reviews.text: This stall is located within the popular havelock road hawker center. Good location as there are ample parking space and most of the stalls are close with many tables available. Overall it’s still a decent mookata, not enough for the 4 star rating but good enough to give it a try. Chili, fresh meat are pretty average and the rest mostly consist of frozen food.

reviews.author: Tan Yeh Hong Joseph
reviews.text:
Located in a food centre with ample parking lots as they are only opened in the evening and during this time, most of the stalls are closed.
Pretty quick service from the friendly uncle. Ordering was easy via a piece of paper. Taste and portion was pretty ok, I would recommend the beef and Thai fish cake.
We went for ala carte and got 11 items. Price was reasonable @ $36 inclusive of GST.

stall_name: Covent Garden Kway Chap (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Kway Chap. I actually wanted to have this long ago but unfortunately the grandma that manages the stall has passed away...
Kway Chap. This is really old school kway chap as all the ingredients are kept warm in a cauldron...
My 2nd time ordering kway chap, and this was much easier given the clear breakdown 😆 Feels and tast...

google.google_name: Covent Garden Kway Chap 哥文園粿汁

reviews.author: chefquak
reviews.text:
had Kovan gardens kway chap

@ havelock hawker centre

$3.50 low price added 50cts small intestines

taste ok.
overall kind of average cannot c/w kian meng @commonwealth crescent.

I guess here got to order few additional items to really compare.

23.1.2026

reviews.author: Bukit Panjang East
reviews.text: Come here for good traditional kway chap. Kway is thin and fresh. All ingredients fresh and well-prepped. Intestines clean and well-braised. Do not miss the chilli sauce. It complements the braised items and gives it a nice kick.

reviews.author: Tengcc Ng
reviews.text: Lovely old school style and taste kway chap. ingredients are freshly prepared early in the morning to be served to their customers around 6.30am. Like their big/small intestines, salted greens, fishcake and their kway which is soft and tasty. Unfortunately, old Ah Mah has retired and no longer serve her regular customers and die hard fans.  Sibeh Ho Jiak!!!!

reviews.author: LP Pua
reviews.text: They don't have a set meal on their menu, but you can still ask them to mix and match for a single portion. The portion was small, but so was the price at just $3.50. They have perfected their Kway Chup—the kway was smooth, and the lightly flavored soup brought back fond memories of how Kway Chup tasted during childhood. They also nailed the small intestine and chili sauce. It's not easy to find small intestines without that awful smell. I regret not ordering a larger portion.

reviews.author: Taste & See
reviews.text: Considered a very good quality kway chap. From the kway (one of the smoothes kway) to the soup to all the ingredients, very well made. But I was kinda surprised by the little amt of ingredients for 1 pax, mixed ingredients add big intestine head with large kway, though it amounted to only $4.50. I decided to add more big intestine head and braised pork belly as the default didn't include pork belly. Total $7.50 still cheap. I like the preserved vegetables though only like 1 piece cut into 3 smaller pieces.

stall_name: Guang Li Wanton Noodle (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Since 1964... old school wanton Mee, nothing spectacular, but it hits the spot...
Once in a while when we need some comfort food. Old school wanton Mee...
Queued up 15-20 min in the line... opened only few...
Wanton Mee

google.google_name: 福客来 Fu Ke Lai Traditional Wanton Mee (#01-27)

reviews.author: Henry Cheng
reviews.text: The noodles were nice, even when packed for takeaway. It has a nice bounciness to it. The sauce was aromatic and mixes really with the noodles. Wantons were a little lumpy in my opinion. Char siew was good. Nice texture and smokiness to it. Overall, good plates of noodles in Havelock food centre.

reviews.author: Cheen the Curious
reviews.text: Wantan mee ($4.50). Rather average bowl. Noodles were ‘q’ (chewy) & drenched in aromatic sauce. Wantans were clumpy, with skin that’s grainy & not the soft type. ‘Char siew’ (BBQ pork) tasted home-made, dark marinade (not the fluorescent red ones) & lightly sweet. Served with a bowl of plain but tasty soup

reviews.author: CK C
reviews.text: Wanton mee was too starchy and one dimensional in taste. The artificially dyed char siew were too sweet. Wantons were OK.

reviews.author: Taste & See
reviews.text: Traditional style. Noodles a bit sticky and alkaline taste. But the sauce is quite nice, green chilli is yummy. Wantons are quite plump but not quite the kind I fancy. soup has some cabbage in it. $4.50 small +$2 wantons

reviews.author: Benito Linus
reviews.text:
Ordered the braised pork rice at $6, (previously $5). Although prices has increased, they give extra ingredients! Now it came with an additional bowl of cold radish salad. And the egg is now 1 whole egg, instead of half an egg last time! The bowl of soup was still the same though.

Anyway the braised pork was still good, very soft and flavourful. Rice was drenched with delicious braised sauce. So good and comforting. Yum 😋 Worth coming back again their food.

stall_name: Hong Kong Jin Tian (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Was introduced to this hawker stall by my colleague whom swears by the Hong Kong roast...
甘榜白切鸡... 鸡肉鲜美，配上独特的姜葱油...
Duck Leg 8nett... Not v crispy, nor super tender, but the flavour is real...
Roast Meat & Char Siew... I told the Cantonese owner they were closed three times...
Lost & Found Popular Roast Meat Stall in Tiong Bahru... Roasted Duck...

google.google_name: Hong Kong Jin Tian Roast Meat

reviews.author: Flo G
reviews.text:
Kudos to authentic Hong Kong chef ( ex-hotel ) who kept the standards and priced reasonably.

Friendly hotel chef is passionate about his food & been running the Jin Tian brand moving between Tiong Bahru and Redhill now settles in havelock.

Ordered wanton mee today and the wanton is really solid, the QQ Al dente noodles are thin hongkong noodles ( the kind I prefer).
Generous with thick slices char siu and enough sauces.

Best part, chef doesn’t charge for takeaway container like his neighbours 🥡

Definitely worth supporting him.

老字号 - trusted old school brand!! Recommend !!

reviews.author: Wei Sern Lee
reviews.text: Visit this store as it’s good review and nearby me and decided to drop by to try out! Love the crispy flavor roast pork that they offer. The chef is from HK and definitely bring out the traditional classic roast meat flavor. Plus point that he’s really friendly and will chitchat with his customers !

reviews.author: Jeremy Matthew
reviews.text: This has got to be one of the best roast pork I’ve had in a while. The skin is thin and absolutely crispy and the meat is not too salty unlike many places I’ve tried. The won ton noodles are even better with chewy noodles and won ton dumplings that are filled with a load of ingredients including crunchy water chestnut that pops in your mouth with each bite. Just $11 for the 2 and it also is worth mentioning that the old chap preparing your meal is really friendly and patient, especially if you converse with him in Cantonese.

reviews.author: Tengcc Ng
reviews.text: If you are looking for authentic hongkong style roast meat, look no further as HongKong Jin Tian retains the HK taste and flavour. Love their roast duck, white chicken, charsiew and roast pork that goes well with either rice or noodle. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Derrick L
reviews.text: Sold by a Cantonese speaking uncle, not sure if he is from hong Kong, the wanton was especially nice, lots of meat and also water chestnut. The rest of the meat - char siew and roasted meat that I tried were pretty average.

stall_name: Ivan's Porridge (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Pork Innards Congee... The minced meat balls are great...
Fish Belly And Pork Porridge... This stall has a variety of Cantonese porridge...
Porridge Or Fried Bee Hoon Your Choice...
Boneless Fish Belly Porridge...

reviews.author: Isaac Sam
reviews.text: One of the most comforting bowls of century egg porridge I’ve had at just $3, it’s an absolute steal. The porridge was smooth, flavourful, and topped generously with spring onions, fried shallots, and ginger. Much much better than many air conditioned restaurants. Every spoonful was warm and satisfying – the perfect way to start the day. Friendly service and no-fuss local charm complete the experience. Highly recommended for a hearty, affordable breakfast.

reviews.author: Derrick L
reviews.text: Great and comforting bowl of porridge in the morning. I ordered the pork porridge with add on - egg and fried intestines for $4. Really reasonable price. The level of water in the porridge is cooked just nice, the raw egg is a perfect combination infused  with the porridge. The fried intestines are crunchy adding another dimension to the porridge. Super friendly auntie and uncle, usually sold out around 1 pm so please be there early.

reviews.author: Jordan M.
reviews.text:
I stopped here after another hawker centre was closed for renovations. I'm a white dude so my experience might be different but I ordered a small bowl of congee for $4. It came out piping hot and so tasty. The auntie and uncle were impressed that I could order in Mandarin but they seem to have a strong following of residents who order from them.

Please come give it a try.

reviews.author: Sean Goh
reviews.text:
Noticed this stall selling cantonese porridge inside Havock road food centre for quite some time. Only managed to try their porridge after the 3rd attempt. They are usually sold out pretty early as 11plus am.

Their menu is quite extensive and price starts from SGD$2.50 onwards. Additional egg and doughsticks  is at 50cents each. Ordered their century egg and pork porridge which was SGD$3.

The porridge here is the cantonese style one. Porridge has a nice consistency and  flavour. The meatball was nicely marinated as well. Their fried intestine porridge also seems to be a crowd favourite as well. Definitely one of the better porridge place i have tried.

reviews.author: Taste & See
reviews.text: Tried the century pork porridge. So good and so smooth. The bee hoon so good too

stall_name: IAAI Malay Food (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: My mee siam is topped with so much bean sprouts, I could not see what lies underneath except for the...

reviews.author: Hatsune Miku
reviews.text: Affordable muslim food starting from $3.50. I went for the nasi lemak and the rice portion was pretty decent. Food came with some kacang, chicken, a hard boiled egg and a small piece of beancurd. Food is not spectacular as the bean curd and chilli was not to my liking. Service is slow as this stall is run by an eldery. Not really a good nasi lemak.

reviews.author: S L
reviews.text:
Had the mee rebus (now an increased $3.5) for breakfast on a quiet Sun morning when the hawker centre, including the stall, had only a handful of customers.

I concur with all the previous positive reviews on the mee rebus. The noodles were silky smooth and was well accentuated by the peanuts and rather big pieces of toufu.

The lady boss makes her food with love in a steady slow manner, so one has to be patient here. And stangely, the queue started forming only after 10am. It seems the Epok-Epok is really popular and many customers order these along with their mains for takeaway.

reviews.author: J Seng
reviews.text: Be patient as the service is on relax pace, but the $0.80 curry puff is delicious and value for money. Nice fluffy crust and tasty curry potato with egg inside.

reviews.author: Popeye Chin (SG Popeye)
reviews.text:
Best Nasi Lemak. Exceptional fragrance nasi lemak. Selling fast! Nasi Lemak start 1000 Come early before 1030 AM before sold out. Taste better  compare to Changi Village Nasi Lemak and Inspirasi.
Mee rebus old style like JB Famous Mee Rebus.

Cash only. Prepare small change. When give $50 note, she was so find offer pay next visit.

Friendly Malay Makcik look like Chinese.
Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre.. IAAI Malay Food #01-23..
Nice..👍
100% Muslim Owned.

reviews.author: Victor Ng
reviews.text:
Be patient. Stall holder is an elderly lady.

Lontong 🫲 rather dilute curry flavour type
Curry puff 👎

stall_name: Covent Garden Prawn Noodle (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: The Dry Version Rocks! It doesn’t get more old-school than this...
Prawn Noodles-$4 One of the perks in staying in a mature estate in Singapore...
Prawns Only Mee With Added Pig's Tail...
Prawn Mee With Intestines...
Pig's Tail Soup...
Prawn Noodles With Intestines & Pig Skins...

google.google_name: Covent Garden Prawn Noodle 哥文园虾面

reviews.author: joe fong
reviews.text:
Chance upon this stall, at a hawker with good prawn noodles.

The prawn are very fresh,  3 big prawns that served with generous noodles..

Can see the soup is done with quality, I would say a good flavoured prawn noodles that will come back again .

Some may say price high, but if this is a food court standard less the aircon , this is price well.

Worth the price no matter what.

The stall is organised and clean, let us support our hawker.

reviews.author: Tengcc Ng
reviews.text: The prawn noodles are so addictive I can eat this almost everyday. Freshly peeled big prawns that goes well with the well cooked intestines and pig skin. Chilli paste and condiments are given just right amount to add a great taste and flavour to the al dente textured noodle. Sibeh Ho Jiak and highly recommended.

reviews.author: Derrick L
reviews.text: Above average prawn noodles for $5, lots of crispy pork lard! 3 large prawns, where to get?

reviews.author: LP Pua
reviews.text: In a hawker centre with not many stalls open for breakfast, this stall had a queue, which piqued my interest to give it a try. The soup had a strong prawn taste (too strong for me), the noodles were soft, the sauce was ideal for the noodles, and the portions of ingredients were generous. Overall, it was a good and reliable bowl of prawn noodles.

reviews.author: Tan. W
reviews.text: This place came highly recommended by my colleague, who had it for breakfast and brought us here for lunch. I tried the dry prawn mee with a mix of kway teow and yellow noodles. At $5, the portion was just right for me. What stood out were the 3 whole prawns—unlike most prawn mee stalls that slice the prawns in half. They were sizeable and paired with a few slices of pork. The soup was rich and flavorful, best enjoyed while it’s hot!

stall_name: Khin Kee Handmade Fishball Kway Teow ᛫ Sliced Fish Bee Hoon (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: 面薄的调味料刚刚好，尤其是醋的分量，不多也不少...
Fishball Kway Teow Soup... breakfast. Food is good tho not earthshaking...
Handmade Fishballs Mee... all handmade by the owners...
Fishball Mee Pok... originated from Toa Payoh...

reviews.author: Bryan Chihan
reviews.text:
Arrived at 8pm. Ordered #2 (Bak Chor Mee Med, Meepok) and #3 (Fishball Kway Teow Soup, Small). There was a constant stream of customers ordering here.

There was a photo showing the boss uncle making fishballs with his hands. So must test it out.

The BCM chili had a kick and was refreshing, and the vinegar was light and flavorful. There was no mince meat though. I guess the auntie replaced it with more ingredients. Only one piece of pork lard though. The noodles were springy and chewy. The fishball and the fried fishcake both were chewy and had a nice fish flavor, meaning more fish used than flour.

As for the Fishball KwayTeow, the soup was light yet with flavor, and the Kwayteow noodles thicker than what you get elsewhere. It was also chewy yet didn't stick together. The fishballs and fishcake here was similarly good.

Overall very worth it for a total of $9.

reviews.author: GK T
reviews.text: First time to this old row of food stalls. We arrived past 8pm and only a few stalls were open. We saw this stall that has a few people queuing. We ordered fishball meepok dry & keay teow soup. Both look normal but sometimes looks deceive. The vinegar once “chop-stick” mixed wth the noodles, gives life to the dish. The taste is not tart-sour but it has subtle sweetness and with the added chilli paste, the flavour is enhanced. The fishball is so traditional in taste as in texture is firm and you do not taste more “filler” flour but you taste the fish. Same goes for the fishcake. The kway teow is also the “old-school” flat white thicker than the “new” kind of white flat vermicelli.

reviews.author: YL Wong
reviews.text:
When in Havelock area, craving for a good wholesome bowl of fish ball noodles… this is the stall.

Their fish balls are all handmade.
Their noodles are not overly oily. Vs other place. (A plus point for me)
Old school taste… nothing fanciful. But delivers. Unlike other place.., they give 1 or 2 factory made meat ball, 1 pc factory made fish dumpling, or 1 deep fried wanton etc.. to me all these are just distraction.
Sorry, ate half way through then remembered to take pic.. 😅

reviews.author: 74tankee
reviews.text: Had a plate of pork liver BCM at $5. The pork liver is cooked just right and the pork slices is tender. The noodle is al dente and the sauce complementsl it well, not over powering. Simply one of the best BCM I have had tried.

reviews.author: Cynthia Ong
reviews.text: Was lucky that there were no customers here yet when I arrived. Order the small fishball noodles and absolutely love the texture of the fishballs, firm and flavourful. Skip the queues of other stalls and give this stall a try!

stall_name: Eng Huat Fishball Mee (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Fishball Noodles-$3.50. Eng Huat is one of the many legendary stalls in the havelock area. They have...

reviews.author: ALAN TAN HL
reviews.text: where did the Shop gone? still open?

reviews.author: William Soh
reviews.text: Fresh fish balls

stall_name: You Yi Fish Soup (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Tom Yam Fried Fish Soup. Walk all the way till the end corner...
Fried Fish Tom Yum Soup ($4). I have been dreaming of this wonderful dish...
Generous chunks of crispy yet lightly battered fresh fish...
For Long Queue Fish Soup. Braved the queue in the warm weather...
Sliced Fish Soup. Looks like everyone came for their fried fish...

reviews.author: Hatsune Miku
reviews.text: Fish portions are very generous for the price. However, the soup doesn't have much unami, both the tom yum and the normal. The fish soup was the least of my favourites, as I felt that the fish portions were not as generous, probably due to the fact that the fish is batang, and well, batang is expensive. Overall, the food is rather decent, and I enjoyed my food for the few times I was there.

reviews.author: Yap Hoe BENG
reviews.text:
Sept 24, have try this Bee Hoon Kuah with fried fish ($5/-)  about 3 times and each time wanted to do a review of the good taste and worth your while to travel here for lunch. This time round it was as good as always, must do the review. Two buddy lady behind the scene, one take order and the other cook. Food is excellent. Service was as good as you can get. Only let down is the hawker Centre, small a L shape (inverted) there is a toilet at the middle. Would I come again. Definitely.
Oct 24, another round with Peter Teo. Prices maintain & still good.
Feb 5, open today after CNY. Rush to eat again. The Tom yam is heaven. Taste slowly.
Feb 10 eat again.
Feb 25 21, have again
Feb 28 again

reviews.author: Lisa Toh
reviews.text: This is our go-to lunch spot whenever we're in the vicinity. My usual order is tom yum sliced fish noodles ($5.50). Generous serving of thick slices of fish. I like it that it's customisable too - tom yum less spicy and noodles #3 (you mian) for me. There are about 5-6 diff types of noodles and stall aunties are patient enough to guide you thru ordering. 😊

reviews.author: Flo Yeow
reviews.text:
They're located at the corner of the food centre, which makes things easier to find. For the price, you cannot really complain. The portions they serve are extremely generous. Had the tom yum fried fish with rice, and thoroughly enjoyed my meal.

The fried fish is extremely crispy, fresh, and meaty. No questions asked, probably one of the best fried fishes that I've had in Singapore.  They don't even mix the fish up into the soup, which says a lot about the effort they put into ensuring the fish doesn't end up soggy.

The tom yum soup, is fragrant, tangy, and refreshing for a lunch meal. One down side is probably the weather, this soup will be super appropriate for a cold rainy day, or if you're enjoying it in an air conditioned room. If not, take it slow and steady.

The rice is probably also something you'll see on numerous tables. It's not a simple white rice, but they do it with vegetables, dried shrimps, and some soya sauce. Not the easiest to find these days in hawker centres, but they still have it served here.

reviews.author: Henry Cheng
reviews.text: Hearty bowl of fish soup. I personally lean more towards the Tom yum soup version. It was delicious and satisfying to eat on a rainy day especially. The additional fried fish was the highlight for the meal. The fish was freshly fried, with a crispy coating on the outside while still remaining soft and tender on the inside. Really worthwhile to pay a visit to this stall.

stall_name: Meng Kee Fried Kway Teow (Havelock Road Cooked Food Centre)
reviews: Lovely version. Well deserving of their recognition. There's rlly no weakness, good wok hei...
Meng kee char kway teow has been hyped up recently on social media...
Char Kway Teow (⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️) When cravings hit hard, you gotta satisfy them...
Better. I didn't mean to eat this again, but it is what it is...

google.google_name: Meng Kee Fried Kway Teow 明記炒粿條

reviews.author: chefquak
reviews.text:
char kway teow very good.👍

has wok heh. very tasty, and a big serving for S$4.

I added cockles S$2. cockles very big and good serving full of plum cockles.

there were crispy lard in large pieces. this not as good as broken up crispy lard fried in the kway teow. would have better better for bites and taste.

7.2.2026

reviews.author: Theng Seng Tan
reviews.text: Old school flavors, modern-day cravings. Meng Kee Fried Kway Teow is still hitting all the right notes. We’re talking about perfectly charred noodles, a generous amount of lap cheong (Chinese sausage), and that signature sweet-savory sauce that coats every strand. It’s not just a meal; it’s a smoky, stir-fried hug.

reviews.author: Jay Jay
reviews.text:
Crave for CKT, specially come here to have the famous MK CKT.  Start q @ 11.30am n food ready at 11.52am.

Ordered the $4/- plus $2/- cockles.  Saw the $5/- n $4/- portion looks almost the same.

The texture is a bit wet as compared to LJ CKT.  Bean sprout portion is huge.  While the cockles are lightly cooked.

Overall, the MK CKT tastes better.

reviews.author: I am getting fat!
reviews.text:
I want my noodles wet, sweet, and glossy enough to see my future in them. 🍜✨

Meng Kee at Havelock Road is the gold standard for this style. The kway teow here is a dark, luscious, caramel-coated masterpiece. Every bite is a smoky explosion of dark soy sauce, crunchy lard, savory lupcheong and plump cockles that haven't been overcooked into rubber. This is one full plate of sin that's worth committing.

I’ve posted some tips on visiting this stall on my Instagram @iamgettingfat. Do check it out :)

reviews.author: geraldine a
reviews.text:
Uncle is friendly, auntie cooking is skilled and the food is great.

I got the 5$ with extra egg. Did not expect such a big portion. Was pleasantly surprised.

This ckt is more sweet than others I’ve tried but it’s still very good.
I would recommend this stall for people who are a fan of the more bloody hum taste as there is plenty and still pretty raw but very plump and juicy.

Delicious even after it’s cold.

Only downside is the waiting.
